touches.ai_summary: Cold outreach to Ryan Lee at ProtonForm introducing our conversation intelligence platform as a solution for rep productivity. Highlighted automation capabilities and ROI potential for mid-market professional services teams.
next_steps: Ryan to review materials and respond if interested

touches.ai_summary: Initial qualification call with Ryan. He's evaluating conversation intelligence tools to improve rep productivity across 24-seat sales team. Currently manual process for call reviews. Budget authority confirmed, timeline is Q2.

questions_asked.question: What's driving you to look at conversation intelligence now?
questions_asked.answer: We're scaling the team and need better coaching infrastructure

questions_asked.question: How does your pricing compare to Chorus?
questions_asked.answer: We're competitive on per-seat basis and include unlimited automation in all tiers

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email with pricing deck and case study from similar professional services company. Confirmed discovery call scheduled for Tuesday.

questions_asked.question: Do you have references in professional services?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, shared two customer stories in the deck

touches.ai_summary: Deep discovery into current state. Ryan described manual QA process, inconsistent coaching, and lack of visibility into deal health. Team uses Salesforce but no conversation layer. Interested in automation for post-call summaries and next steps.

questions_asked.question: Walk me through your current call review process
questions_asked.answer: Manager listens to 2-3 calls per rep per month, very manual

questions_asked.question: What metrics matter most for your team?
questions_asked.answer: Talk-listen ratio, discovery question depth, objection handling

questions_asked.question: Can we integrate with Salesforce automatically?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, bidirectional sync included

next_steps: Ryan to map out current tech stack

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up qualification call to confirm technical requirements. Discussed Salesforce integration, Zoom/Teams recording setup, and user provisioning. Ryan confirmed he has authority to make the decision but wants to loop in sales leadership before final approval.

questions_asked.question: Who else should be involved in the evaluation?
questions_asked.answer: VP Sales will need to sign off, but Ryan owns the vendor selection

questions_asked.question: What's the implementation timeline?
questions_asked.answer: Typically 2 weeks for technical setup, 4 weeks to full adoption

next_steps: Schedule deeper discovery on use cases

touches.ai_summary: Discovery focused on specific use cases. Ryan emphasized need for automated rep scorecards and deal risk identification. Also exploring competitor analysis features. Discussed Chorus as alternative but Ryan concerned about their pricing model and feature limitations.

questions_asked.question: What would success look like 6 months after launch?
questions_asked.answer: Every rep getting weekly coaching insights, managers saving 10+ hours/month

questions_asked.question: How do your automation limits compare to Chorus?
questions_asked.answer: We include unlimited automation workflows, Chorus caps at their tier structure

questions_asked.question: Can we customize scoring criteria?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, fully configurable talk tracks and scoring rubrics

next_steps: Ryan to share sample calls for demo prep

touches.ai_summary: Quick check-in call. Ryan confirmed sample calls sent and reiterated timeline pressure to get something in place by end of quarter. Asked about onboarding support.

questions_asked.question: What does onboarding look like?
questions_asked.answer: Dedicated CSM for first 60 days, weekly check-ins, custom playbook setup

touches.ai_summary: Pre-demo alignment call. Reviewed ProtonForm's specific talk tracks and coaching priorities. Ryan shared that VP Sales is skeptical of new tools after a failed sales enablement platform last year, so demo needs to be highly relevant.

questions_asked.question: What went wrong with the previous tool?
questions_asked.answer: Poor adoption, too complex, didn't integrate well

questions_asked.question: What will make the VP say yes?
questions_asked.answer: Clear ROI, simple UX, proven track record

touches.ai_summary: Extended discovery to build business case. Calculated current cost of manual coaching (manager time) and missed revenue from inconsistent deal execution. Ryan estimated 15-20% of deals slip due to poor discovery. Discussed metrics to track post-implementation.

questions_asked.question: How much manager time goes to call coaching today?
questions_asked.answer: Roughly 8-10 hours per manager per week

questions_asked.question: What's your average deal size and win rate?
questions_asked.answer: $18K ACV, 22% win rate

questions_asked.question: Can we track win rate improvement in your platform?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have deal outcome analytics tied to conversation quality

next_steps: Build custom ROI model for ProtonForm

touches.ai_summary: First demo session focused on core conversation intelligence features. Walked through call recording, AI summarization, and automated scorecards using ProtonForm's actual talk tracks. Ryan impressed by automation quality and Salesforce sync. Asked detailed questions about reporting and manager dashboards.

questions_asked.question: Can managers see real-time alerts for at-risk deals?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, demonstrated deal health scoring and alert workflows

questions_asked.question: How accurate is the AI summarization?
questions_asked.answer: Showed 3 sample calls with 95%+ accuracy on key points

questions_asked.question: What's the learning curve for reps?
questions_asked.answer: Minimal - most features are automatic, reps just review their scorecards

touches.ai_summary: Second demo covering advanced features: custom playbooks, competitor tracking, and deal risk analysis. Ryan particularly interested in tracking mentions of Chorus and other competitors. Discussed reporting for VP Sales and integration with existing BI tools. Ryan noted he'll need to present to leadership solo since VP is traveling.

questions_asked.question: Can we export data to our existing dashboards?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, API access and direct data warehouse integration available

questions_asked.question: How long until we see ROI?
questions_asked.answer: Most customers see coaching efficiency gains within 30 days, win rate impact by 90 days

questions_asked.question: When can you present to the VP?
questions_asked.answer: He's traveling for 2 weeks, Ryan will present internally with our materials

next_steps: Send presentation deck for internal champion use

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email with demo recordings, ROI model showing $180K 3-year value, and champion enablement deck for Ryan to present to VP Sales. Included competitive comparison vs Chorus highlighting unlimited automation and pricing advantage.

questions_asked.question: Can you include a reference call option in the proposal?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, connecting you with similar-sized professional services customer

next_steps: Ryan presenting to VP next week

touches.ai_summary: Proposal review call. Walked through 24-seat quote at $20K ACV with unlimited automation tier. Ryan asked about discount options to help with internal budget approval. Discussed payment terms and contract length. He mentioned VP had positive reaction to materials but wants to move quickly.

questions_asked.question: Is there flexibility on pricing for annual commit?
questions_asked.answer: We can explore discount options based on contract terms

questions_asked.question: What's your budget range?
questions_asked.answer: Approved for $25K but want to stay under $20K if possible

objections: Pricing needs to be competitive with Chorus quote they received

touches.ai_summary: Brief exec alignment call with Ryan. VP Sales has questions but delegated evaluation to Ryan. Timeline compressed - need to close by end of month for Q2 kickoff. Ryan pushing for additional discount to get under $20K to avoid VP approval threshold.

questions_asked.question: What's the best you can do on price?
questions_asked.answer: Proposed 24.6% discount to hit $19,999 - just under approval threshold

questions_asked.question: Can we get VP on a call to align on strategy?
questions_asked.answer: He's delegated this fully to me, trusts my recommendation

touches.ai_summary: Extended exec meeting to finalize business case. Ryan walked through VP feedback - generally positive but concerned about adoption risk given previous tool failure. Discussed change management plan and success metrics. Ryan needs to present final recommendation with contract by end of week.

questions_asked.question: What would de-risk this for the VP?
questions_asked.answer: Strong onboarding plan and quarterly business reviews

questions_asked.question: Can we do a pilot first?
questions_asked.answer: Pricing is already aggressive, full deployment recommended for ROI

questions_asked.question: What if adoption doesn't hit targets?
questions_asked.answer: We can include success metrics in the contract terms

objections: VP worried about user adoption based on past experience

touches.ai_summary: Email with revised proposal at $19,999, detailed onboarding plan, and draft MSA. Emphasized unlimited automation value vs Chorus limitations and included adoption success framework. Reference call feedback attached.

Negotiation call on contract terms. Procurement flagged auto-renewal clause and data retention policies. Ryan advocating internally but needs flexibility on terms. Also asked about reducing seat count to lower price further.

questions_asked.question: Can we remove auto-renewal?
questions_asked.answer: We can do 60-day notice period instead

questions_asked.question: What if we start with 20 seats?
questions_asked.answer: Price increases on per-seat basis, 24 seats gets better rate

questions_asked.question: Is the VP fully aligned?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, this is just procurement doing their job

objections: Procurement pushing back on standard contract terms

touches.ai_summary: Exec meeting to resolve contract blockers. Discussed data security requirements and SOC2 compliance. Ryan confirmed VP approved the deal but procurement needs security questionnaire completed. Timeline slipping to early next month.

questions_asked.question: Can you complete security review this week?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, our standard security packet covers most requirements

questions_asked.question: Any other stakeholders we need to involve?
questions_asked.answer: IT security will review, but Ryan can push it through

touches.ai_summary: Extended exec session to finalize all terms. Reviewed security feedback (approved), negotiated final payment terms (annual upfront vs quarterly), and confirmed implementation timeline. Ryan mentioned Chorus reached out with competitive offer but he's committed to our solution. VP still hasn't joined any calls directly.

questions_asked.question: Can we do quarterly payments?
questions_asked.answer: Annual upfront required for this discount level

questions_asked.question: What's the Chorus offer?
questions_asked.answer: Similar price but Ryan prefers our automation and UX

questions_asked.question: When can we get VP on a call to close?
questions_asked.answer: Not necessary, Ryan has full authority to sign

objections: Payment terms pushback on annual commitment

touches.ai_summary: Quick negotiation call on final pricing adjustment request. Ryan asked for additional 5% discount to secure faster approval. Discussed trade-offs and reaffirmed value at current pricing.

questions_asked.question: Can you do $18.5K to make this easier?
questions_asked.answer: Already at 24.6% discount, that's our floor for this seat count

touches.ai_summary: Final negotiation call. Ryan confirmed $19,999 approved, contracts going to legal for signatures. Discussed implementation kickoff timeline and CSM assignment. Targeting go-live in 3 weeks.

questions_asked.question: Who will be our CSM?
questions_asked.answer: Assigning Sarah Chen, 5 years experience with professional services customers

questions_asked.question: When can we expect signatures?
questions_asked.answer: Should be signed by end of week
